description: cs_assure impact policy: repo-relative path globs -> obligations that FIRE when a matching path appears in the change set. OBSERVATION-ONLY - fired obligations are reported for the engineer/agent to discharge; the engine never enforces, gates, auto-fixes, or blocks. Each obligation id equals a .claude/rules/<id>.md filename stem and its globs are kept EQUAL to that rule's `paths:` front-matter by test_impact_policy_matches_rules, so the machine change->obligation map and the read-time rules never drift. Invariant-derived (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) obligations that are not yet backed by a rule file are a documented future extension (promote them to a real rule first, then add them here under the same equality test).

obligations.obligation: BOOTSTRAP_SELF_MODIFIED: this change edits the tool that judges changes, so its own assurance output is candidate-local and provisional; admit only via the repo's pre-existing gate + CI + INDEPENDENT human review, never self-approve or self-merge, and keep the kernel stdlib-only and fail-closed (exit 2).
obligations.authority: the repo's pre-existing gate + CI + an independent human reviewer (NOT the assurance system's own output).

obligations.obligation: Regenerate the JSON schemas + TS types and update the three contract-count assertions in tests/test_platform_contracts.py in the same change; keep every foundational contract dense-safe / MoE-compatible.
obligations.authority: CI diffs the regenerated output; a trigger-independent backstop test byte-compares the committed schemas + TS types.

obligations.obligation: An unavailable metric is null-with-typed-reason, never a fabricated 0.0; exclude infrastructure-failure rows from the quality denominator so a broken backend never reads as a bad model.
obligations.authority: the honesty invariant upheld across the scorers, the aggregate report, and the model card.

obligations.obligation: LOOP_CONTROLLER_SELF_MODIFIED: this change edits the autonomous loop controller, which itself decides retry / stop / escalate / hold / merge / finalize - so the loop's own run is NOT independent verification of a change to it. Admit only via trusted-base controller tests + CI + INDEPENDENT human review; NO autonomous merge and no self-approval (a loop-controller change never merges via the loop's own merge gate); keep the controller stdlib-only, fail-closed, and injected-effect (no real effect hardcoded).
obligations.authority: trusted-base controller tests + CI + an independent human reviewer (NOT the loop's own run / merge gate).

obligations.obligation: Append-only, immutable, auth-gated overlay: never edit a frozen protocol/amendment/matrix in place, never reword sealed<->exploratory, and on a spec/on-disk conflict STOP and escalate rather than reconcile.
obligations.authority: validate_protocol.py green; explicit human authorization per auth-gated action; escalation to the research authority.

obligations.obligation: A change to worker-execution bytes needs a fresh pinned worker package + new environment locks; trace the import path and write the WORKER_CHANGE_REQUIRED vs control-plane-only verdict (this matcher flags only the DECLARED worker files, not lazily-reachable modules - the trace is still the engineer's).
obligations.authority: a written symbol-level import trace; a fresh pinned worker package + env locks (sealed research adds the amendment/matrix/reserved-id/rebuilt-wheel/-vN chain).
